feature_name: Publish calendar board day-week drag build to hub beta
scope_summary: Publishes a new hub beta app build that adds a day/week calendar board with drag-based task scheduling and wires calendar entry points into Today/Tasks navigation.

legal_and_regulatory.rationale: Feature remains within productivity tooling scope and legal disclosures continue to be accessible.
evidence: No regulated medical feature was introduced
Existing legal/privacy routes remain available in the app shell

privacy_local_first_promise.rationale: Scheduling changes stay local-first and do not introduce new network data flow.
evidence: Calendar scheduling writes to the existing local task store
No new API endpoints or third-party SDK integrations were added

medical_scope_and_claims.rationale: Copy remains behavioral/productivity-focused with no diagnosis or treatment claims.
evidence: Calendar feature copy references planning and task flow only

behavioral_science_alignment.rationale: Visual day/week placement plus drag rescheduling reduces friction for next-action planning.
evidence: Users can drag tasks between days instead of editing dates in forms
Unscheduled inbox drop zone supports low-friction date clearing

neurodivergence_research_alignment.rationale: The board supports chunking and temporal scaffolding without increasing cognitive overload.
evidence: Day/week modes allow reduced information density
Today/Tasks links to calendar keep the anchor-task workflow intact

user_benefit_and_harm_check.rationale: Users gain a direct scheduling surface and can quickly rebalance workload without losing tasks.
evidence: Calendar route includes drag scheduling and unschedule drop zone
Automated tests and build passed before publish

brand_voice_and_claims.rationale: UI copy keeps direct, non-manipulative Divergify voice and avoids exaggerated outcomes.
evidence: Route labels and helper text remain operational and plain-language
No marketing claims were introduced by this build

accessibility_and_inclusion.rationale: Calendar includes button-based fallback actions alongside drag interactions for broader access.
evidence: Tasks can be scheduled with explicit buttons, not drag only
Navigation exposes calendar from tabs and planner actions

security_and_reliability.rationale: Build artifacts were regenerated from typed source and validated with tests/build checks.
evidence: TypeScript + Vite production build completed successfully

monetization_and_dark_pattern_check.rationale: No monetization mechanics were added and no coercive prompts were introduced.
evidence: Calendar release only updates planner functionality and navigation
No purchase/paywall/upsell UI or conversion logic changed

ship_decision.conditions: Deploy and verify /hub/beta loads with the new /calendar route and drag scheduling flow.
